topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sound of the phone buzzing again was the final straw. I slammed the laptop shut, the sharp click echoing in the quiet living room. I walked over to Leo’s bedroom door, my jaw tight. "Seriously, Leo? Another secret life?" I leaned against the frame, crossing my arms so tightly my knuckles were white. I could practically feel the simmering frustration rising in my chest, a hot, prickly sensation behind my eyes. I knew he was hiding something, but the sheer audacity of it all was grating on my nerves. I pushed the door open without knocking. The screen was lit, and I saw it—a profile I didn't recognize, filled with carefully curated images and cryptic captions.

[story 2]

I watched him scroll through his phone, a slow, deliberate motion that felt like a deliberate act of defiance. Every tap seemed to pull more layers of unspoken things into the light, and it made my stomach clench uncomfortably. I sighed, a long, drawn-out sound that carried the weight of my exasperation. "Put the device down, Leo," I managed, my voice carefully level, though the edge in it was unmistakable. I felt a distinct tightening in my shoulders, a physical manifestation of my mounting displeasure. When he finally looked up, his eyes darted away, and the quick, furtive glance confirmed everything. That little corner of his digital world, hidden away from me, was clearly causing a significant amount of trouble.

[story 3]

It started with a misplaced login detail, a slip of the finger that led me down a rabbit hole of hidden feeds and private posts. I paced the hallway, the floorboards seeming to amplify the restless energy churning inside me. Every time I glanced toward his room, a knot formed in my chest, a heavy, unwelcome pressure. I didn't want to start a confrontation, but the sheer secrecy was exhausting. "I just need to understand what this is," I stated, my tone clipped and devoid of warmth. I watched as he tried to shut down the notifications, his movements jerky, clearly uncomfortable under the scrutiny. The silence stretched, thick and uncomfortable, punctuated only by the faint, rhythmic tapping of his thumb on the screen.
